docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
FMU Manipulation Toolbox is a comprehensive Python toolkit designed to analyze, modify, and combine Functional Mock-up Units (FMUs) without requiring recompilation. Built for engineers and developers working with FMI-compliant models, it provides three powerful interfaces:

Graphical InterfaceCommand LinePython API 
fmutool-gui

Perfect for interactive, visual manipulation of FMUs with an intuitive point-and-click interface.

fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-toplevel -output module-flat.fmu

Ideal for automation, scripting, and integration into build pipelines.

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")
fmu.apply_operation(OperationStripTopLevel())
fmu.repack("module-flat.fmu")

Full programmatic control for complex workflows and custom integrations.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• FMU Analysis

    • List all ports and their attributes
    • Validate modelDescription.xml against XSD schema
    • Check FMI compliance
    • Extract statistics and metadata

  • FMU Modification

    • Rename ports individually or in batch via CSV
    • Filter variables with regular expressions
    • Remove hierarchy levels
    • Modify variable attributes
    • Remove sources

  • FMU Containers

    • Combine multiple FMUs into one
    • Define automatic or explicit connections
    • Multi-threading support
    • Performance profiling

  • Binary Interfaces

    • Add 32-bit interface to 64-bit FMU (Windows)
    • Add 64-bit interface to 32-bit FMU (Windows)
    • Frontend mode for separate process execution

docs.title: FMI Versions
docs.text:
• FMI 2.0
  • FMI 3.0

docs.title: Operating Systems
docs.text:
• Windows 10/11 (primary platform)
  • Linux (Ubuntu 22.04+)
  • macOS (Darwin)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install with a single command:

pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

Virtual Environment Recommended

For production use, we recommend installing in a virtual environment to avoid dependency conflicts:

python -m venv fmu_env
source fmu_env/bin/activate  # On Windows: fmu_env\Scripts\activate
pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

For detailed installation instructions, platform-specific notes, and troubleshooting, see the Installation Guide.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
Here's how to analyze and modify an FMU in just a few lines:

PythonCommand Line 
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

# Load and analyze
fmu = FMU("my_model.fmu")
fmu.summary()

# Remove internal variables
fmu.variables = [v for v in fmu.variables if not v.name.startswith('_')]

# Save modified FMU
fmu.repack("my_model_clean.fmu")

# Analyze FMU
fmutool -input my_model.fmu -summary

# Remove internal variables
fmutool -input my_model.fmu \
  -remove-regexp "^_.*" \
  -output my_model_clean.fmu

# Verify result
fmutool -input my_model_clean.fmu -check

docs.title: Why FMU Manipulation Toolbox?
docs.text:
Key Benefits

No Recompilation Required Modify FMUs without access to source code or compilation toolchain Multiple Interfaces Choose the interface that fits your workflow: GUI, CLI, or Python API Production Ready Battle-tested in automotive and industrial applications Open Source BSD-2-Clause license allows both commercial and non-commercial use Well Documented Comprehensive documentation with 50+ examples and use cases

docs.title: Command Line Interface (CLI) Usage Guide
docs.text: The command line offers complete and precise control over FMU Manipulation Toolbox, ideal for automation and batch processing.

docs.title: Available Commands
docs.text:
FMU Manipulation Toolbox provides three main commands:

Command Usage fmutool FMU analysis and manipulation fmucontainer FMU container creation fmusplit Extract FMUs from a container

docs.title: Basic Syntax
docs.text: fmutool -input <file.fmu> [options]

docs.title: Required Options
docs.text:
-input path/to/module.fmu

The FMU to analyze or modify.

docs.title: Save Options
docs.text:
-output path/to/module-modified.fmu

⚠️ Important: Without this option, no modifications are saved!

docs.title: Display FMU Summary
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu -summary

Output: 

FMU Information:
  Name: module
  FMI Version: 2.0
  GUID: {abc-123-def}
  Number of variables: 42
  Number of parameters: 5
  Number of inputs: 10
  Number of outputs: 15

docs.title: Check FMI Compliance
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu -check

Output: 

Checking FMU: module.fmu
✓ modelDescription.xml is valid
✓ Schema validation passed
⚠ Warning: Variable 'temp' has no description
✓ All value references are unique

docs.title: List All Ports in CSV
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu -dump-csv ports_list.csv

Generated CSV content: 

name;newName;valueReference;causality;variability
Motor.Speed;Motor.Speed;0;input;continuous
Motor.Torque;Motor.Torque;1;output;continuous
Controller.Kp;Controller.Kp;2;parameter;fixed

docs.title: Remove Top-Level Hierarchy
docs.text:
Before: 

System.Motor.Speed
System.Controller.Gain

Command: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-toplevel -output module-flat.fmu

After: 

Motor.Speed
Controller.Gain

docs.title: Merge First Level
docs.text:
Before: 

System.Motor.Speed
System.Controller.Gain

Command: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -merge-toplevel -output module-merged.fmu

After: 

System_Motor.Speed
System_Controller.Gain

docs.title: Remove Prefix
docs.text:
Before: 

_internal_Motor_Speed
_internal_Controller_Gain

Command: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -trim-until _ -output module-trimmed.fmu

After: 

internal_Motor_Speed
internal_Controller_Gain

docs.title: Rename from CSV File
docs.text:
Step 1: Create CSV 

fmutool -input module.fmu -dump-csv renaming.csv

Step 2: Edit CSV

Edit renaming.csv: 

name;newName;valueReference;causality;variability
Motor.Speed;Engine.Velocity;0;input;continuous
Motor.Torque;Engine.Force;1;output;continuous

Step 3: Apply modifications 

fmutool -input module.fmu -rename-from-csv renaming.csv -output module-renamed.fmu

Tip: Leaving newName empty removes the port!

docs.title: Filter by Regular Expression
docs.text:
Keep only matching ports: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -keep-only-regexp "^Motor\..*" -output module-motor.fmu

Remove matching ports: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-regexp "^Internal\..*" -output module-clean.fmu

docs.title: Regular Expression Examples
docs.text:
# Keep everything starting with "Motor."
-keep-only-regexp "^Motor\..*"

# Remove everything containing "debug"
-remove-regexp ".*debug.*"

# Keep ports ending with "Temperature"
-keep-only-regexp ".*Temperature$"

# Remove ports starting with underscore
-remove-regexp "^_.*"

docs.title: Remove All Ports
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-all -output module-empty.fmu

⚠️ Warning: This removes ALL ports! Use with filters.

docs.title: Operations on Parameters Only
docs.text:
List only parameters: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -only-parameters -dump-csv parameters.csv

Remove all parameters: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -only-parameters -remove-all -output module-no-params.fmu

docs.title: Operations on Inputs Only
docs.text:
# List inputs
fmutool -input module.fmu -only-inputs -dump-csv inputs.csv

# Rename inputs
fmutool -input module.fmu -only-inputs -rename-from-csv new_inputs.csv -output module.fmu

docs.title: Operations on Outputs Only
docs.text:
# List outputs
fmutool -input module.fmu -only-outputs -dump-csv outputs.csv

# Filter outputs
fmutool -input module.fmu -only-outputs -keep-only-regexp "^Result\." -output module.fmu

docs.title: Remove Sources
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-sources -output module-no-src.fmu

Effect: The sources/ folder is removed from the FMU.

docs.title: Add Binary Interface (Windows)
docs.text:
Add 64-bit interface to 32-bit FMU: 

fmutool -input module32.fmu -add-remoting-win64 -output module-dual.fmu

Add 32-bit interface to 64-bit FMU: 

fmutool -input module64.fmu -add-remoting-win32 -output module-dual.fmu

docs.title: Combining Operations
docs.text: Options can be combined for complex transformations:

docs.title: Example 1: Clean and Simplify
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu \
  -remove-regexp "^_internal.*" \
  -remove-toplevel \
  -remove-sources \
  -output module-clean.fmu

docs.title: Example 2: Filter and Rename
docs.text:
fmutool -input module.fmu \
  -keep-only-regexp "^Motor\." \
  -rename-from-csv motor_names.csv \
  -output module-motor-renamed.fmu

docs.title: Basic Syntax
docs.text: fmucontainer -container config.csv -fmu-directory ./fmus

docs.title: Main Options
docs.text:
-container filename.csv          # Configuration file
-fmu-directory path/to/fmus      # Directory containing FMUs
-fmi {2|3}                       # FMI version (default: 2)
-output container.fmu            # Container name (optional)

docs.title: Configuration CSV Format
docs.text:
fmu;input;output
motor.fmu;controller.command;sensor.speed
controller.fmu;sensor.speed;motor.command
sensor.fmu;motor.torque;controller.feedback

docs.title: Advanced Options
docs.text:
# Enable multi-threading
fmucontainer -container system.csv -fmu-directory ./fmus -mt

# Enable profiling
fmucontainer -container system.csv -fmu-directory ./fmus -profile

# Expose parameters
fmucontainer -container system.csv -fmu-directory ./fmus -auto-parameter

# Debug mode
fmucontainer -container system.csv -fmu-directory ./fmus -debug

docs.title: Simple Bash Script
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash

# Process all FMUs in a directory
for fmu in *.fmu; do
  echo "Processing $fmu..."
  fmutool -input "$fmu" \
    -remove-toplevel \
    -remove-sources \
    -output "processed_$fmu"
done

echo "All FMUs processed!"

docs.title: Batch Script (Windows)
docs.text:
@echo off
for %%f in (*.fmu) do (
  echo Processing %%f...
  fmutool -input "%%f" -remove-toplevel -output "processed_%%f"
)
echo All FMUs processed!

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text:
# Analysis
fmutool -input X.fmu -summary              # Summary
fmutool -input X.fmu -check                # Verification
fmutool -input X.fmu -dump-csv list.csv    # List ports

# Simple modification
fmutool -input X.fmu -remove-toplevel -output Y.fmu
fmutool -input X.fmu -merge-toplevel -output Y.fmu

# Renaming
fmutool -input X.fmu -rename-from-csv names.csv -output Y.fmu

# Filtering
fmutool -input X.fmu -keep-only-regexp "PATTERN" -output Y.fmu
fmutool -input X.fmu -remove-regexp "PATTERN" -output Y.fmu

# By type
fmutool -input X.fmu -only-parameters -dump-csv params.csv
fmutool -input X.fmu -only-inputs -dump-csv inputs.csv
fmutool -input X.fmu -only-outputs -dump-csv outputs.csv

# Binaries (Windows)
fmutool -input X.fmu -add-remoting-win64 -output Y.fmu
fmutool -input X.fmu -remove-sources -output Y.fmu

# Containers
fmucontainer -container config.csv -fmu-directory ./fmus

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• 🐍 Python API - Advanced automation
  • 💡 Examples - Detailed use cases
  • 🆘 Troubleshooting

docs.title: Common Use Cases
docs.text: This guide presents practical solutions to frequently encountered problems when manipulating FMUs.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Migration and Renaming
  • Cleaning and Simplification
  • Extraction and Filtering
  • Validation and Quality Control
  • Portability and Distribution

docs.title: Case 1: Change Naming Convention
docs.text:
Problem: Convert from CamelCase to snake_case for all ports.

CLI Solution:

# 1. Export list
fmutool -input OldModel.fmu -dump-csv old_names.csv

# 2. Use Python to convert
python convert_names.py old_names.csv new_names.csv

# 3. Apply
fmutool -input OldModel.fmu -rename-from-csv new_names.csv -output new_model.fmu

Python Script convert_names.py:

import pandas as pd
import re

def camel_to_snake(name):
    """Convert CamelCase to snake_case."""
    s1 = re.sub('(.)([A-Z][a-z]+)', r'\1_\2', name)
    return re.sub('([a-z0-9])([A-Z])', r'\1_\2', s1).lower()

# Read CSV
df = pd.read_csv('old_names.csv', sep=';')

# Convert names
df['newName'] = df['name'].apply(camel_to_snake)

# Save
df.to_csv('new_names.csv', sep=';', index=False)
print("✓ Conversion complete")

docs.title: Case 2: Prefix All Ports
docs.text:
Problem: Add Component1_ prefix to all ports before integration.

Python API Solution:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Add prefix
for var in fmu.variables:
    var.name = f"Component1_{var.name}"

fmu.repack("module_prefixed.fmu")

docs.title: Case 3: Remove Debug Variables
docs.text:
Problem: Remove all debug variables before distribution.

CLI Solution:

# Remove everything containing "debug" or "test"
fmutool -input model_dev.fmu \
  -remove-regexp ".*[Dd]ebug.*" \
  -remove-regexp ".*[Tt]est.*" \
  -output model_release.fmu

Python API Solution:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

fmu = FMU("model_dev.fmu")

# Filter variables
fmu.variables = [
    v for v in fmu.variables 
    if not any(keyword in v.name.lower() for keyword in ['debug', 'test', '_tmp'])
]

fmu.repack("model_release.fmu")

docs.title: Case 4: Simplify Deep Hierarchy
docs.text:
Problem: Reduce Level1.Level2.Level3.Variable hierarchy to Level3.Variable.

Python API Solution:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

fmu = FMU("deep.fmu")

for var in fmu.variables:
    parts = var.name.split('.')
    if len(parts) > 2:
        # Keep only last 2 levels
        var.name = '.'.join(parts[-2:])

fmu.repack("flat.fmu")

docs.title: Case 5: Extract Only Tunable Parameters
docs.text:
Problem: Create FMU containing only adjustable parameters.

CLI Solution:

fmutool -input complete_model.fmu \
  -only-parameters \
  -dump-csv parameters.csv

Python API Solution:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

fmu = FMU("complete_model.fmu")

# Keep only tunable parameters
fmu.variables = [
    v for v in fmu.variables 
    if v.causality == "parameter" and v.variability == "tunable"
]

fmu.repack("tunable_parameters.fmu")

docs.title: Case 6: Create Specialized Variants
docs.text:
Problem: Create 3 FMUs from one: engine, controller, sensors.

Python API Solution:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationKeepOnlyRegexp

# Configuration
variants = {
    "engine": r"^Engine\..*",
    "controller": r"^Controller\..*",
    "sensors": r"^Sensor\..*"
}

base_fmu = "vehicle_complete.fmu"

for name, pattern in variants.items():
    # Load base FMU
    fmu = FMU(base_fmu)

    # Filter
    operation = OperationKeepOnlyRegexp(pattern)
    fmu.apply_operation(operation)

    # Save variant
    fmu.repack(f"{name}.fmu")
    print(f"✓ Created: {name}.fmu with {len(fmu.variables)} variables")

docs.title: Case 7: Complete FMU Audit
docs.text:
Problem: Verify compliance and generate report.

Python API Solution:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU
import json
from datetime import datetime

def audit_fmu(fmu_path, report_path):
    """Generate complete audit report."""
    fmu = FMU(fmu_path)

    # Collect information
    report = {
        "audit_date": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "fmu_path": fmu_path,
        "basic_info": {
            "name": fmu.name,
            "guid": fmu.guid,
            "fmi_version": fmu.fmi_version
        },
        "statistics": {
            "total_variables": len(fmu.variables),
            "parameters": fmu.count_parameters(),
            "inputs": fmu.count_inputs(),
            "outputs": fmu.count_outputs()
        },
        "validation": {
            "xml_valid": fmu.validate_model_description()
        },
        "warnings": []
    }

    # Additional checks
    if len(fmu.variables) == 0:
        report["warnings"].append("No variables defined")

    # Variables without description
    no_desc = [v.name for v in fmu.variables if not getattr(v, 'description', '')]
    if no_desc:
        report["warnings"].append(f"{len(no_desc)} variables without description")

    # Save report
    with open(report_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(report, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

    print(f"✓ Report generated: {report_path}")

# Usage
audit_fmu("model.fmu", "audit_report.json")

docs.title: Case 8: CI/CD Pipeline Validation
docs.text:
Problem: Automatically validate FMUs in GitLab CI.

.gitlab-ci.yml:

validate_fmus:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.10
  script:
    - pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox
    - python validate_all.py
  artifacts:
    reports:
      junit: test-results.xml

validate_all.py:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU
from pathlib import Path
import sys

def validate_all_fmus(directory):
    """Validate all FMUs in directory."""
    fmu_dir = Path(directory)
    all_valid = True

    for fmu_file in fmu_dir.glob("**/*.fmu"):
        print(f"\nValidating {fmu_file.name}...")

        try:
            fmu = FMU(str(fmu_file))

            if fmu.validate_model_description():
                print(f"  ✓ {fmu_file.name} is valid")
            else:
                print(f"  ✗ {fmu_file.name} is invalid")
                all_valid = False

        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  ✗ Error loading: {e}")
            all_valid = False

    return 0 if all_valid else 1

if __name__ == "__main__":
    exit_code = validate_all_fmus("./fmus")
    sys.exit(exit_code)

docs.title: Case 9: Add Multi-Platform Support (Windows)
docs.text:
Problem: 32-bit FMU must also work in 64-bit.

CLI Solution:

fmutool -input model_win32.fmu \
  -add-remoting-win64 \
  -output model_dual.fmu

docs.title: Case 10: Prepare FMU for Distribution
docs.text:
Problem: Completely clean FMU before public distribution.

Complete Solution:

fmutool -input internal_model.fmu \
  -remove-regexp "^_.*" \
  -remove-regexp ".*[Dd]ebug.*" \
  -remove-regexp ".*[Tt]est.*" \
  -remove-regexp "^Internal\..*" \
  -remove-sources \
  -output public_model.fmu

# Verify
fmutool -input public_model.fmu -check

docs.title: Case 11: Process Multiple FMUs
docs.text:
Python Script:

from pathlib import Path
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

def batch_process(input_dir, output_dir):
    """Process all FMUs in directory."""
    input_path = Path(input_dir)
    output_path = Path(output_dir)
    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    for fmu_file in input_path.glob("*.fmu"):
        print(f"Processing {fmu_file.name}...")

        try:
            fmu = FMU(str(fmu_file))

            # Apply transformation
            operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
            fmu.apply_operation(operation)

            # Save
            output_file = output_path / fmu_file.name
            fmu.repack(str(output_file))

            print(f"  ✓ Success")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  ✗ Error: {e}")

# Usage
batch_process("./input", "./output")

docs.title: Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
1. Always keep original - Never overwrite source FMU
  2. Test progressively - Apply and verify one modification at a time
  3. Validate after each step - Use -check or validate_model_description()
  4. Document transformations - Create reusable scripts
  5. Version FMUs - Use version control system

docs.title: Pre-Distribution Checklist
docs.text:
• Remove internal variables (^_.*)
  • Remove debug variables
  • Remove sources if necessary
  • Check FMI compliance (-check)
  • Test in target environment
  • Generate documentation
  • Version the FMU

For more examples and support: - 📚 Complete Documentation - 🐛 GitHub Issues

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will get you up and running with FMU Manipulation Toolbox in less than 15 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure you have:

  • Python 3.7 or higher
  • pip (Python package manager)
  • An FMU file to work with (or use our examples)

What is an FMU?

A Functional Mock-up Unit (FMU) is a standardized format for model exchange and co-simulation defined by the FMI Standard. FMUs are widely used in automotive, aerospace, and industrial automation.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install FMU Manipulation Toolbox using pip:

pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

Verify the installation:

fmutool --help
Installation successful!

You should see the help message displaying all available commands. If you encounter issues, check our Troubleshooting Guide.

docs.title: Your First FMU Analysis
docs.text: Let's start by analyzing an existing FMU to understand its structure.

docs.title: Step 1: Get FMU Information
docs.text:
Command LinePython APIGraphical Interface 
fmutool -input my_module.fmu -summary

Expected Output: 

FMU Information:
  Name: my_module
  FMI Version: 2.0
  GUID: {12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}
  Number of variables: 42
  Number of parameters: 5
  Number of inputs: 8
  Number of outputs: 12


from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

# Load the FMU
fmu = FMU("my_module.fmu")

# Display summary
fmu.summary()

# Access specific information
print(f"FMI Version: {fmu.fmi_version}")
print(f"Total Variables: {len(fmu.variables)}")
print(f"Parameters: {fmu.count_parameters()}")

fmutool-gui
  1. Click Load FMU
  2. Select your FMU file
  3. View the summary in the interface

docs.title: Step 2: List All Ports
docs.text:
Export all port information to a CSV file for easy viewing:

fmutool -input my_module.fmu -dump-csv ports_list.csv

Open ports_list.csv to see all ports with their attributes:

name causality variability type valueReference Motor.Temperature output continuous Real 0 Motor.Speed input continuous Real 1 Controller.Gain parameter fixed Real 2

Pro Tip: Excel Integration

Open the CSV in Excel or LibreOffice Calc for easy filtering and sorting. Use semicolon (;) as the delimiter.

docs.title: Your First FMU Modification
docs.text: Now let's modify an FMU by simplifying its port hierarchy.

docs.title: Problem: Complex Hierarchy
docs.text:
Your FMU has this structure:

System.Motor.Temperature
System.Motor.Speed
System.Controller.Gain

You want to simplify it to:

Motor.Temperature
Motor.Speed
Controller.Gain

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Command LinePython APIGraphical Interface 
fmutool -input my_module.fmu \
  -remove-toplevel \
  -output my_module_simplified.fmu

Verify the result:

fmutool -input my_module_simplified.fmu -summary

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

# Load FMU
fmu = FMU("my_module.fmu")

# Apply transformation
operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
fmu.apply_operation(operation)

# Save result
fmu.repack("my_module_simplified.fmu")
  1. Load your FMU
  2. Click Strip Toplevel button
  3. Click Save and choose output filename

Important: Original FMU Preservation

The original FMU is never modified. All operations create a new copy, ensuring data safety.

docs.title: Batch Renaming with CSV
docs.text: For complex renaming operations, use CSV files.

docs.title: Step 1: Generate Renaming Template
docs.text: fmutool -input my_module.fmu -dump-csv renaming.csv

docs.title: Step 2: Edit the CSV
docs.text:
Open renaming.csv and modify the newName column:

Before: 

name;newName;valueReference;causality;variability
Motor.Temp;Motor.Temp;0;output;continuous
Motor.RPM;Motor.RPM;1;input;continuous

After: 

name;newName;valueReference;causality;variability
Motor.Temp;Engine_Temperature;0;output;continuous
Motor.RPM;Rotation_Speed;1;input;continuous

Empty newName = Port Removal

Leave newName empty to remove a port from the FMU.

docs.title: Step 3: Apply Renaming
docs.text:
fmutool -input my_module.fmu \
  -rename-from-csv renaming.csv \
  -output my_module_renamed.fmu

docs.title: Common Operations
docs.text:
Here are the most frequently used operations:

  • Filter Ports

    Keep only ports matching a pattern:

    fmutool -input module.fmu \
  -keep-only-regexp "^Motor\..*" \
  -output module_motor.fmu

  • Remove Internals

    Remove all internal variables:

    fmutool -input module.fmu \
  -remove-regexp "^Internal\..*" \
  -output module_clean.fmu

  • Validate FMU

    Check FMI compliance:

    fmutool -input module.fmu -check

  • Extract Parameters

    List only parameters:

    fmutool -input module.fmu \
  -only-parameters \
  -dump-csv parameters.csv

docs.title: Essential Commands Reference
docs.text: Command Description -summary Display FMU information -check Validate FMI compliance -dump-csv file.csv Export ports to CSV -remove-toplevel Remove top hierarchy level -rename-from-csv file.csv Batch rename from CSV -keep-only-regexp "pattern" Keep matching ports only -remove-regexp "pattern" Remove matching ports

docs.title: Quick Troubleshooting
docs.text:
FMU Won't Load

Check the FMU validity: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -check

Common causes: - Corrupted ZIP archive - Invalid modelDescription.xml - Missing required files

Modifications Not Applied

Did you forget the -output option?

❌ Wrong: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-toplevel

✅ Correct: 

fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-toplevel -output module_flat.fmu

CSV Renaming Error

Ensure your CSV file:

  • Is UTF-8 encoded
  • Uses semicolon (;) as delimiter
  • Contains all required columns: name;newName;...
  • Includes all ports from the original FMU

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you've mastered the basics, explore more advanced features:

  • CLI Guide

    Master command-line operations

  • Python API

    Automate with Python scripts

  • GUI Guide

    Use the graphical interface

  • Use Cases

    Real-world examples

Ready to Go!

You're now equipped with the fundamentals of FMU Manipulation Toolbox. Happy modeling! 🎉

Need help? Check our FAQ or report an issue.

docs.title: Graphical User Interface (GUI) Usage Guide
docs.text: The graphical interface of FMU Manipulation Toolbox offers an intuitive and visual way to manipulate your FMUs without using the command line.

docs.title: Launching the Interface
docs.text: fmutool-gui

docs.title: Interface Overview
docs.text: The graphical interface is organized into several sections:

docs.title: 1. Loading Area (Top)
docs.text:
• Load FMU: Button to load an FMU from your disk
  • Display of loaded FMU name

docs.title: 2. Visualization Area (Center)
docs.text:
• List of all FMU ports with their attributes:
  • Name
  • Type (Input, Output, Parameter, Local)
  • Data Type (Real, Integer, Boolean, String)
  • Causality
  • Variability

docs.title: 3. Action Area (Right and Bottom)
docs.text:
• Color-coded buttons for different operations
  • Filters to limit scope of operations

docs.title: 4. Save Area (Bottom)
docs.text:
• Save: Button to save modified FMU
  • Operation status display

docs.title: Button Color Code
docs.text:
The interface uses an intuitive color code:

Color Action Type Examples 🔴 Red Remove information Remove ports, Remove sources 🟠 Orange Modify modelDescription.xml Rename, Strip toplevel 🟢 Green Add components or check Add remoting, Check FMU 🟣 Purple Extract and save Extract descriptor, Save CSV 🔵 Blue Filter scope or exit Filter by type, Exit

docs.title: Step 1: Load an FMU
docs.text:
1. Click Load FMU
  2. Navigate to your .fmu file
  3. Select the file and validate

Result: The port list displays in the central area.

docs.title: Step 2: Explore Ports
docs.text:
The list displays all important information:

Name                    | Causality | Variability | Type
------------------------|-----------|-------------|------
Motor.Temperature       | output    | continuous  | Real
Motor.Speed             | input     | continuous  | Real
Controller.Gain         | parameter | fixed       | Real

List Features: - Sort: Click column headers to sort - Search: Use Ctrl+F to search in list - Selection: Click a row to select it

docs.title: 🟣 Export Port List to CSV
docs.text:
Use Case: Prepare a file for renaming ports

  1. Click Export to CSV
  2. Choose location and filename
  3. Click Save

Result: A CSV file with all ports is created.

docs.title: 🟠 Remove Top-Level Hierarchy
docs.text:
Use Case: Simplify name hierarchy

Before: 

System.Motor.Temperature
System.Motor.Speed
System.Controller.Gain

Action: 1. Click Strip Toplevel 2. Confirm action

After: 

Motor.Temperature
Motor.Speed
Controller.Gain

docs.title: 🟠 Rename Ports from CSV
docs.text:
Prerequisite: Have a CSV file with name;newName columns

  1. Click Rename from CSV
  2. Select your CSV file
  3. Validate

The FMU is updated with new names!

docs.title: 🔴 Remove Ports
docs.text:
Option 1: Remove by Regular Expression

  1. Click Remove by RegExp
  2. Enter regular expression (e.g., ^Internal\..*)
  3. Validate

Option 2: Remove All Ports of a Type

  1. Use filters (blue buttons) to select type
  2. Only Parameters
  3. Only Inputs
  4. Only Outputs
  5. Click Remove All
  6. Confirm

docs.title: 🟢 Add Binary Interface (Windows Only)
docs.text:
Use Case: Add 64-bit interface to 32-bit FMU

  1. Click Add Remoting Win64
  2. Wait for processing to complete
  3. The FMU can now be used in both 32 and 64 bits

Also Available: - Add Remoting Win32: Add 32-bit interface - Add Frontend Win32/Win64: Run FMU in separate process

docs.title: 🟢 Check FMU
docs.text:
Use Case: Validate FMU compliance with FMI standard

  1. Click Check FMU
  2. View results in status area

Possible Results: - ✅ Valid: FMU is compliant - ⚠️ Warnings: FMU has minor issues - ❌ Errors: FMU has major issues

docs.title: Step 4: Filter Operations
docs.text: Blue buttons allow limiting action scope:

docs.title: 🔵 Filter by Port Type
docs.text:
Only Parameters: Operations apply only to parameters

Usage Example: 1. Click Only Parameters 2. Click Export to CSV 3. Only parameters are exported

Other Filters: - Only Inputs - Only Outputs

docs.title: 🔵 Reset Filters
docs.text: Click Clear Filters to return to full view.

docs.title: Step 5: Save Modified FMU
docs.text:
⚠️ IMPORTANT: The original FMU is never modified.

  1. Click Save
  2. Choose name and location for new FMU
  3. Validate

The new FMU contains all your modifications!

docs.title: Example 1: Simplify FMU Structure
docs.text:
Objective: Remove VehicleModel. prefix from all ports

Steps: 1. Load FMU → VehicleModel.fmu 2. Strip Toplevel 3. Save → VehicleModel_simplified.fmu

Result: - Before: VehicleModel.Engine.Temperature - After: Engine.Temperature

docs.title: Example 2: Export Only Parameters
docs.text:
Objective: Create CSV file with only parameters

Steps: 1. Load FMU → module.fmu 2. Only Parameters (filter) 3. Export to CSV → parameters.csv

docs.title: Example 3: Clean Internal Variables
docs.text:
Objective: Remove all variables starting with _internal

Steps: 1. Load FMU → module.fmu 2. Remove by RegExp 3. Enter: ^_internal.* 4. Validate 5. Save → module_clean.fmu

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Ctrl+O Open an FMU Ctrl+S Save FMU Ctrl+F Search in list Ctrl+Q Quit application F5 Refresh view

docs.title: ✅ Do
docs.text:
• Always save with a new name to keep original intact
  • Check FMU after important modifications (Check FMU button)
  • Test modified FMU in your simulation environment before production use
  • Document your modifications by noting applied transformations

docs.title: ❌ Avoid
docs.text:
• Don't chain too many operations without intermediate saves
  • Don't modify without backup of original
  • Don't delete essential ports without checking dependencies
  • Don't forget to save before closing the application

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
For FMUs with >5000 variables, the interface may be slow.

Solution: Use command line or Python API for better performance.

docs.title: Complex Operations
docs.text: Some advanced operations are only available via command line: - FMU Containers (fmucontainer) - Automation scripts - Batch processing

docs.title: Interface Won't Launch
docs.text:
Problem: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tkinter'

Solution: 

# Linux
sudo apt-get install python3-tk

# macOS
brew install python-tk

docs.title: Interface is Frozen
docs.text: Solution: 1. Close application 2. Relaunch with smaller FMU 3. Or use command line

docs.title: Going Further
docs.text:
For more advanced operations:

  • 📖 CLI Guide - Command line usage
  • 🐍 Python API - Automation with scripts
  • 💡 Examples - Advanced use cases

docs.title: Support
docs.text: For any questions or issues: - 📚 Troubleshooting - 🐛 GitHub Issues

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: This guide walks you through installing FMU Manipulation Toolbox on your system.

docs.title: Supported Operating Systems
docs.text:
• ✅ Windows 10/11 (primary platform, fully tested)
  • ✅ Linux (Ubuntu 22.04 and compatible distributions)
  • ✅ macOS (Darwin)

docs.title: Required Dependencies
docs.text:
• Python 3.7 or higher
  • pip (Python package manager)

docs.title: Optional Dependencies (for compilation from source)
docs.text:
• C Compiler compatible with C99 or later
  • Windows: Visual Studio 2019+ or MinGW
  • Linux: GCC 9+ or Clang
  • macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools
  • CMake 3.20 or higher

docs.title: Method 1: Installation via PyPI (Recommended)
docs.text: This is the simplest and fastest method to install FMU Manipulation Toolbox.

docs.title: Standard Installation
docs.text: pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

docs.title: Installation with Upgrade
docs.text: pip install --upgrade fmu-manipulation-toolbox

docs.title: Installing a Specific Version
docs.text: pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox==1.9.1

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
# Check installed version
pip show fmu-manipulation-toolbox

# Test commands
fmutool -h
fmutool-gui
fmucontainer -h

Expected Output: 

Name: fmu-manipulation-toolbox
Version: 1.9.1
Summary: FMU Manipulation Toolbox
Home-page: https://github.com/grouperenault/fmu_manipulation_toolbox
Author: Renault
License: BSD-2-Clause

docs.title: Method 2: Installation from Source
docs.text: This method is recommended if you want to: - Contribute to the project - Use the latest development version - Modify the source code

docs.title: Step 1: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/grouperenault/fmu_manipulation_toolbox.git
cd fmu_manipulation_toolbox

docs.title: Step 2: Install Python Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt

Contents of requirements.txt: 

lxml>=4.9.0
numpy>=1.21.0

docs.title: On Windows
docs.text:
With Visual Studio: 

mkdir build
cd build
cmake .. -G "Visual Studio 16 2019" -A x64
cmake --build . --config Release
cd ..

With MinGW: 

mkdir build
cd build
cmake .. -G "MinGW Makefiles"
cmake --build .
cd ..

docs.title: On Linux
docs.text:
mkdir build
cd build
cmake ..
make
cd ..

docs.title: On macOS
docs.text:
mkdir build
cd build
cmake ..
make
cd ..

docs.title: Development Mode Installation (changes take effect immediately)
docs.text: pip install -e .

docs.title: Standard Installation
docs.text: pip install .

docs.title: Installation in a Virtual Environment (Recommended)
docs.text: Using a virtual environment avoids dependency conflicts.

docs.title: With venv (Python standard)
docs.text:
# Create virtual environment
python -m venv fmu_env

# Activate environment
# On Windows:
fmu_env\Scripts\activate
# On Linux/macOS:
source fmu_env/bin/activate

# Install FMU Manipulation Toolbox
pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

# Deactivate environment (when done)
deactivate

docs.title: With conda
docs.text:
# Create environment
conda create -n fmu_env python=3.10

# Activate environment
conda activate fmu_env

# Install FMU Manipulation Toolbox
pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

# Deactivate environment
conda deactivate

docs.title: Issue: pip install fails
docs.text:
Error: error: Microsoft Visual C++ 14.0 or greater is required

Windows Solution: 1. Install Visual Studio Build Tools 2. Or install a pre-compiled version: pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox --only-binary :all:

Linux Solution: 

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install python3-dev build-essential

macOS Solution: 

xcode-select --install

docs.title: Issue: Commands not found after installation
docs.text:
Solution:

Verify that the Python scripts directory is in your PATH:

# Find the directory
python -m site --user-base

# Add to PATH
# Windows: Add %APPDATA%\Python\Python3X\Scripts
# Linux/macOS: Add ~/.local/bin to your PATH

docs.title: Issue: Permission denied (Linux/macOS)
docs.text:
Solution: 

# Option 1: User installation
pip install --user fmu-manipulation-toolbox

# Option 2: With sudo (not recommended)
sudo pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

docs.title: Check Available Version
docs.text:
pip search fmu-manipulation-toolbox
# Or visit: https://pypi.org/project/fmu-manipulation-toolbox/

docs.title: Update to Latest Version
docs.text: pip install --upgrade fmu-manipulation-toolbox

docs.title: Downgrade to Previous Version
docs.text: pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox==1.9.0

docs.title: Uninstallation
docs.text:
pip uninstall fmu-manipulation-toolbox

For complete uninstallation (with dependencies):

pip uninstall fmu-manipulation-toolbox lxml numpy

docs.title: Supported Python Versions
docs.text: Python Version Support 3.7 ✅ Supported 3.8 ✅ Supported 3.9 ✅ Supported 3.10 ✅ Supported (Recommended) 3.11 ✅ Supported 3.12 ⚠️ In testing

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that FMU Manipulation Toolbox is installed, you can:

  1. 🚀 Follow the Getting Started Guide
  2. 📖 Consult the CLI Usage Guide
  3. 🎨 Discover the Graphical Interface
  4. 🐍 Explore the Python API

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
If you encounter installation issues:

  1. Check the Troubleshooting Guide
  2. Review GitHub Issues
  3. Create a new issue with your system details and error message

docs.title: Python API Usage Guide
docs.text: The Python API of FMU Manipulation Toolbox allows you to fully automate your FMU manipulation tasks and integrate them into your workflows.

docs.title: Installation and Import
docs.text:
# Installation
# pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

# Import
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import (
    FMU,
    OperationStripTopLevel,
    OperationRenameFromCSV,
    OperationSaveNamesToCSV,
    OperationRemoveRegexp,
    OperationKeepOnlyRegexp
)

docs.title: Load an FMU
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

# Load the FMU
fmu = FMU("path/to/module.fmu")

# Display summary
fmu.summary()

# List variables
for var in fmu.variables:
    print(f"{var.name}: {var.causality} - {var.variability}")

docs.title: Save a Modified FMU
docs.text:
# After modifications
fmu.repack("path/to/module-modified.fmu")

⚠️ Important: The original FMU is never modified. repack() creates a new copy.

docs.title: FMU Properties
docs.text:
# General information
print(f"Name: {fmu.name}")
print(f"GUID: {fmu.guid}")
print(f"FMI Version: {fmu.fmi_version}")
print(f"Description: {fmu.description}")

# Counters
print(f"Number of variables: {len(fmu.variables)}")
print(f"Number of parameters: {fmu.count_parameters()}")
print(f"Number of inputs: {fmu.count_inputs()}")
print(f"Number of outputs: {fmu.count_outputs()}")

docs.title: Access Variables
docs.text:
# All variables
all_vars = fmu.variables

# Filter by causality
parameters = [v for v in fmu.variables if v.causality == "parameter"]
inputs = [v for v in fmu.variables if v.causality == "input"]
outputs = [v for v in fmu.variables if v.causality == "output"]

# Search for specific variable
motor_speed = fmu.get_variable("Motor.Speed")
if motor_speed:
    print(f"Found: {motor_speed.name}")
    print(f"  Type: {motor_speed.type}")
    print(f"  Causality: {motor_speed.causality}")

docs.title: Remove Top-Level Hierarchy
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

# Load FMU
fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Create and apply operation
operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
fmu.apply_operation(operation)

# Save
fmu.repack("module-flat.fmu")

Effect: - Before: System.Motor.Speed - After: Motor.Speed

docs.title: Export Names to CSV
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationSaveNamesToCSV

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Create operation
operation = OperationSaveNamesToCSV()

# Apply (collect names)
fmu.apply_operation(operation)

# Write CSV
operation.write_csv("ports_list.csv")

docs.title: Rename from CSV
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationRenameFromCSV

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Load and apply renamings
operation = OperationRenameFromCSV("renaming.csv")
fmu.apply_operation(operation)

# Save
fmu.repack("module-renamed.fmu")

docs.title: Filter by Regular Expression
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationKeepOnlyRegexp

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Keep only Motor.* ports
operation = OperationKeepOnlyRegexp(r"^Motor\..*")
fmu.apply_operation(operation)

fmu.repack("module-motor-only.fmu")

Remove by regexp:

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationRemoveRegexp

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Remove all Internal.* ports
operation = OperationRemoveRegexp(r"^Internal\..*")
fmu.apply_operation(operation)

fmu.repack("module-clean.fmu")

docs.title: Chain Multiple Operations
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import (
    FMU,
    OperationRemoveRegexp,
    OperationStripTopLevel,
    OperationRenameFromCSV
)

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Operation 1: Remove internal variables
op1 = OperationRemoveRegexp(r"^_internal.*")
fmu.apply_operation(op1)

# Operation 2: Simplify hierarchy
op2 = OperationStripTopLevel()
fmu.apply_operation(op2)

# Operation 3: Rename
op3 = OperationRenameFromCSV("new_names.csv")
fmu.apply_operation(op3)

# Save final result
fmu.repack("module-transformed.fmu")

docs.title: Conditional Manipulation
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")

# Create renaming dictionary
renaming_map = {}

for var in fmu.variables:
    # Rename only parameters
    if var.causality == "parameter":
        new_name = f"param_{var.name}"
        renaming_map[var.name] = new_name

    # Add prefix to outputs
    elif var.causality == "output":
        new_name = f"out_{var.name}"
        renaming_map[var.name] = new_name

# Apply renamings
for old_name, new_name in renaming_map.items():
    var = fmu.get_variable(old_name)
    if var:
        var.name = new_name

fmu.repack("module-prefixed.fmu")

docs.title: Batch Processing Script
docs.text:
import os
from pathlib import Path
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import (
    FMU,
    OperationStripTopLevel,
    OperationRemoveRegexp
)

def process_fmu(input_path, output_path):
    """Process a single FMU."""
    try:
        # Load
        fmu = FMU(input_path)

        # Clean
        op1 = OperationRemoveRegexp(r"^_internal.*")
        fmu.apply_operation(op1)

        # Simplify
        op2 = OperationStripTopLevel()
        fmu.apply_operation(op2)

        # Save
        fmu.repack(output_path)

        print(f"✓ Processed: {input_path}")
        return True

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"✗ Error with {input_path}: {e}")
        return False

def process_directory(input_dir, output_dir):
    """Process all FMUs in a directory."""
    input_path = Path(input_dir)
    output_path = Path(output_dir)

    # Create output directory
    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Process each FMU
    success_count = 0
    fail_count = 0

    for fmu_file in input_path.glob("*.fmu"):
        output_file = output_path / f"processed_{fmu_file.name}"

        if process_fmu(str(fmu_file), str(output_file)):
            success_count += 1
        else:
            fail_count += 1

    print(f"\n=== Summary ===")
    print(f"Successful: {success_count}")
    print(f"Failed: {fail_count}")

# Usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    process_directory("./input_fmus", "./output_fmus")

docs.title: Parallel Processing
docs.text:
from multiprocessing import Pool
from pathlib import Path
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

def process_single_fmu(args):
    """Function to process one FMU (for multiprocessing)."""
    input_path, output_path = args

    try:
        fmu = FMU(input_path)
        operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
        fmu.apply_operation(operation)
        fmu.repack(output_path)
        return (input_path, True, None)
    except Exception as e:
        return (input_path, False, str(e))

def parallel_processing(input_dir, output_dir, num_workers=4):
    """Process FMUs in parallel."""
    input_path = Path(input_dir)
    output_path = Path(output_dir)
    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Prepare arguments
    tasks = []
    for fmu_file in input_path.glob("*.fmu"):
        output_file = output_path / f"processed_{fmu_file.name}"
        tasks.append((str(fmu_file), str(output_file)))

    # Process in parallel
    with Pool(num_workers) as pool:
        results = pool.map(process_single_fmu, tasks)

    # Display results
    for fmu_path, success, error in results:
        if success:
            print(f"✓ {fmu_path}")
        else:
            print(f"✗ {fmu_path}: {error}")

# Usage
parallel_processing("./input_fmus", "./output_fmus", num_workers=4)

docs.title: Validation in CI/CD Pipeline
docs.text:
from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU
import sys

def validate_fmu(fmu_path):
    """Validate an FMU and return exit code."""
    try:
        fmu = FMU(fmu_path)

        # Checks
        is_valid = fmu.validate_model_description()

        if not is_valid:
            print(f"✗ ERROR: {fmu_path} is not valid")
            print(fmu.get_validation_errors())
            return 1

        # Additional checks
        if len(fmu.variables) == 0:
            print(f"⚠ WARNING: {fmu_path} has no variables")
            return 1

        print(f"✓ SUCCESS: {fmu_path} is valid")
        print(f"  - {len(fmu.variables)} variables")
        print(f"  - {fmu.count_parameters()} parameters")

        return 0

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"✗ ERROR: Cannot load {fmu_path}")
        print(f"  {e}")
        return 1

# Usage in CI/CD
if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python validate.py <fmu_file>")
        sys.exit(1)

    exit_code = validate_fmu(sys.argv[1])
    sys.exit(exit_code)

docs.title: ✅ Do
docs.text:
# Always verify loading
try:
    fmu = FMU("module.fmu")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Loading error: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)

# Validate after important modifications
fmu.apply_operation(operation)
if not fmu.validate_model_description():
    print("Warning: Invalid FMU after modification")

# Use absolute paths to avoid issues
from pathlib import Path
fmu_path = Path("module.fmu").resolve()
fmu = FMU(str(fmu_path))

docs.title: ❌ Avoid
docs.text:
# ❌ Forgetting to apply operation
operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
# fmu.apply_operation(operation)  # FORGOTTEN!
fmu.repack("output.fmu")  # No changes!

# ❌ Modifying original FMU
fmu = FMU("original.fmu")
fmu.apply_operation(operation)
fmu.repack("original.fmu")  # DANGER! Never overwrite original

# ✅ Always create new file
fmu.repack("original_modified.fmu")

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• 💡 Advanced Examples
  • 🆘 Troubleshooting

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
• 📖 Complete Documentation
  • 🐛 GitHub Issues

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: This guide helps you resolve common issues with FMU Manipulation Toolbox.

docs.title: ❌ pip install fails with compilation error
docs.text:
Symptom: 

error: Microsoft Visual C++ 14.0 or greater is required

Solutions:

Windows: 

# Option 1: Install Visual Studio Build Tools
# Download from: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/downloads/

# Option 2: Use pre-compiled version
pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox --only-binary :all:

Linux: 

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install python3-dev build-essential cmake
pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

macOS: 

xcode-select --install
pip install fmu-manipulation-toolbox

docs.title: ❌ Command fmutool not found
docs.text:
Symptom: 

'fmutool' is not recognized as an internal or external command

Cause: Python scripts directory is not in PATH.

Solution:

Windows: 

# Find scripts path
python -m site --user-base
# Add %APPDATA%\Python\Python3X\Scripts to your PATH

# Or use directly:
python -m fmu_manipulation_toolbox.fmutool -h

Linux/macOS: 

# Add to PATH in ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Reload
source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: ❌ FMU cannot be loaded
docs.text:
Symptom: 

Error: Unable to load FMU: [path/to/file.fmu]

Checks:

# 1. Verify file exists
ls -l my_module.fmu

# 2. Verify it's a valid ZIP file
unzip -t my_module.fmu

# 3. Check FMU structure
fmutool -input my_module.fmu -check

Solutions:

  1. Corrupted FMU: Get a fresh copy
  2. Incorrect extension: Verify the file has .fmu extension
  3. Permissions: Check file access rights

docs.title: ❌ Invalid modelDescription.xml
docs.text:
Symptom: 

Error: Invalid modelDescription.xml
XMLSyntaxError: ...

Diagnosis: 

# Extract and check XML file
fmutool -input my_module.fmu -extract-descriptor modelDescription.xml

# Open with editor to see errors
cat modelDescription.xml

docs.title: ❌ Modifications not applied
docs.text:
Symptom: Output FMU is identical to original.

Cause: Missing -output option

Solution: 

# ❌ INCORRECT (no save)
fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-toplevel

# ✅ CORRECT
fmutool -input module.fmu -remove-toplevel -output module-modified.fmu

docs.title: ❌ CSV renaming error
docs.text:
Symptom: 

Error: Port 'xyz' not found in CSV file

Solutions:

  1. Incorrect CSV format
# Generate correct CSV first
fmutool -input module.fmu -dump-csv ports.csv

# Verify format (must contain: name;newName;...)
head ports.csv
  1. File encoding

CSV must be UTF-8 encoded with semicolon (;) separator

  1. Missing names in CSV
# Regenerate complete CSV
fmutool -input module.fmu -dump-csv ports_complete.csv

docs.title: ❌ Name collision after modification
docs.text:
Symptom: 

Error: Duplicate variable name after renaming: 'Temperature'

Cause: Two different variables have the same new name.

Solution:

Check your renaming CSV: 

# ❌ INCORRECT (two variables → same name)
Motor.Temperature;Temperature;0;output;continuous
Controller.Temperature;Temperature;1;output;continuous

# ✅ CORRECT (distinct names)
Motor.Temperature;Motor_Temperature;0;output;continuous
Controller.Temperature;Controller_Temperature;1;output;continuous

docs.title: ❌ GUI won't launch
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tkinter'

Solution:

Linux (Ubuntu/Debian): 

sudo apt-get install python3-tk

macOS (Homebrew): 

brew install python-tk

Windows: Tkinter is normally included. Reinstall Python with tcl/tk option.

docs.title: ❌ Operations don't modify the FMU
docs.text:
Wrong code: 

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")
operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
# ❌ Operation not applied!
fmu.repack("output.fmu")

Correct code: 

from fmu_manipulation_toolbox.operations import FMU, OperationStripTopLevel

fmu = FMU("module.fmu")
operation = OperationStripTopLevel()
fmu.apply_operation(operation)  # ✅ Apply the operation!
fmu.repack("output.fmu")

docs.title: Need More Help?
docs.text:
1. Check the complete documentation
  2. Search GitHub Issues
  3. Create a new issue with system details and error message

Last updated: February 2026
